title: Rebase, Sequencer, Autosquash, and Topology Preservation · Command Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs rebase, sequencer, autosquash, and topology preservation from terse repository evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the rebase todo, mapping, and equivalence proof. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for rebase todo, mapping, and equivalence proof after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model rebase as commit selection, temporary reset, ordered replay, new object creation, and ref movement rather than branch movement magic.
Use --onto, upstream, branch, fork-point, and explicit revision sets to select exactly which commits move and where.
Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, exec, drop, break, label, reset, and merge actions while preserving dependency order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Gita Rebase Replay Model evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Gita Rebase Replay Model assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Case G57 is a review in a disposable repository. the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity. The learner must predict range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries, run git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD, explain four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review, test this changed condition: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge, and preserve this recovery boundary: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Gita Rebase Replay Model has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Gita Rebase Replay Model precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G57 is a review in a disposable repository. the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity. The learner must predict range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries, run git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD, explain four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review, test this changed condition: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge, and preserve this recovery boundary: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Rebase moves existing commit objects to new parents without changing their IDs.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Record what required effort

content.title: Evidence defense
